lenses.guidance: vv 1-15's deception-narrative pivots on v 14's literary key — 'they did not inquire of the LORD.' The structural failure is procedural; the chapter teaches that covenant-making without divine inquiry binds Israel to its mistakes.

vv 15-27's Gibeonite preservation — sworn oath kept despite the deception — shows covenant integrity outweighing tactical advantage. The redemption arc honors oaths even when extracted by trickery; God's people keep their word.

v 15's Gibeonite-oath threads to 2 Sam 21:1-9, where Saul's later violation brings famine on David's reign. The canonical line shows oath-breach echoing forward across centuries; covenant memory is generationally binding.
